extra_model_fields.target_attempt_note: Name the revised attempt 183-revised and include all machine-readable exports and updated trace steps.
verification_steps_for_reviewer: Confirm CSVs contain the same numeric tables as XLSX.

planned_changes: Add explicit numeric recalculations for each recommended action in the calculation_log: show original energy_density and compute energy_density after each recommended parameter change (both power and speed variants when suggested).

Create a sensitivity analysis table that applies +/-2% and +/-5% perturbations to power and speed for each flagged layer, recompute energy_density for each scenario, and report whether the temp/porosity flags would flip; include a short interpretation paragraph summarizing robustness.

Export key workbook sheets as machine-readable CSV files (anomaly_table.csv, decision_table.csv, calculation_log.csv) and include calculation_log.txt (plain text) in the artifact package and link them from final_message.txt.

In the workbook/report, add an assumptions section that documents any empirical or theoretical mapping (or lack thereof) between energy_density and meltpool temperature and advise a monitored test-run if mapping uncertainty remains.
Update trace/step logs to include the new recomputation steps so automated graders/verifiers can re-run/recompute from trace without requiring openpyxl.
raw_llm_output.revision_reason: Address missing numeric post-change calculations, add sensitivity/uncertainty analysis, and improve artifact inspectability for graders without openpyxl.

metadata_json.revision_goal: The baseline submission correctly computed energy density and applied rule-based flags (ri_1 and ri_2 pass). However recommendations lack explicit numeric post-change calculations and there is no numeric sensitivity/uncertainty analysis (ri_3 and ri_4 failed/weak). Additionally, the grader environment could not parse the workbook preview (openpyxl unavailable); include machine-readable exports to ensure inspectability in constrained runtimes.
